THE PROPOSED ORDERS ARE TO BE SUBMITTED TO JUDGE PRO TEM ROGER MEAD FOR REVIEW AND SIGNATURE. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Knowledgeable And Request For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar May-02-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Benjamin Beck, Esq. of Strategic Legal Practices, Tel. 310.929.4904, appearing for plaintiff/moving party Jennifer Dalling;
Nejla Nassirian, Esq. of Universal & Shannon LLP, Tel. 916.780.4050 ext. 104, appearing for defendant/opposing party FCA US LLC.

Both sides stipulate that the matter may be heard by Judge Pro Tem Roger Mead.

Matter called, argument held.

Ruling: At the conclusion of argument, Plaintiff Jennifer Dalling's Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Knowledgeable And Request For Production Of Documents is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART. Each counsel is to submit their respective proposed orders by May 3, 2018 and proposed protective order(s) by May 9, 2018 which are to be consistent with the discussions/arguments held at the hearing. The proposed orders are to be submitted directly to Judge Pro Tem Roger Mead for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Roger Mead, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: David Disbrow, CSR #7768, Tel. 415.298.1538. =(302/JPT/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Knowledgeable And Request For Production Of Documents

MINI MINUTES:

Nejla Nassirian, Esq. of Universal & Shannon LLP, Tel. 916.780.4050, appearing by CourtCall for the Defendant/Opposing Party FCA US LLC.

Both sides stipulate that the matter can be heard by Judge Pro Tem Roger Mead.

Matter called, argument held.

The parties are ordered to meet and confer by telephone and are to report any unresolved issues to Judge Pro Tem Roger Mead by the close of business on April 23, 2018.

Judge Pro Tem: Roger Mead, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: David Disbrow, CSR #7768, Tel. 415.298.1538. =(302/JPT/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Deposition; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-11-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nejla Nassirian, Esq. of Universal & Shannon LLP, Tel. 916.780.4050, appearing by CourtCall for the Defendant/Moving Party FCA US LLC;
Vanessa J. Oliva, Esq. of Strategic Legal Practice, Tel. 310.929.4941, appearing by CourtCall for the Plaintiff/Opposing Party Jennifer Dalling.

Both sides stipulate that the matter can be heard by Judge Pro Tem Thomas A. Cohen.

Matter called, argument held.

Ruling - At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on Defendant FCA US Llc's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Deposition; Request For Sanctions as follows: The Motion is granted. Plaintiff shall appear for her deposition b May 1, 2018 unless the parties mutually agree to a different date. Plaintiff's counsel offered March 2, 2018 as a possible deposition date. Defendant timely and properly noticed the deposition for that date. On February 27, 2018, Plaintiff's counsel stated in an email to Defendant's counsel that she was inclined to pull the deposition unless other demands were met regarding the deposition of Defendant's PMK.. In that email, she never mentioned that she would be unavailable on March 2. When Defendant declined to meet her demands, Plaintiff's counsel announced that she was unavailable.

Sanctions are awarded to Defendant against Plaintiff and her counsel in the amount of $2,325.

Order signed. (See order filed this date for details.)

Judge Pro Tem: Thomas A. Cohen, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, David Disbrow, CSR #7768, Tel. 415.298.1538. (302/JPT/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Vehicle Inspection And Request For Monetary Sanctions [Ccp 2031.310, 2031.010]
Off Calendar Apr-11-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nejla Nassirian, Esq. of Universal & Shannon LLP, Tel. 916.780.4050, appearing by CourtCall for the Defendant/Moving Party FCA US LLC;
Vanessa J. Oliva, Esq. of Strategic Legal Practice, Tel. 310.929.4941, appearing by CourtCall for the Plaintiff/Opposing Party Jennifer Dalling.

Both sides stipulate that the matter can be heard by Judge Pro Tem Thomas A. Cohen.

Matter called, argument held.

Ruling - At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on Defendant FCA US Llc's Motion To Compel Vehicle Inspection And Request For Monetary Sanctions [CCP 2031.310, 2031.010] as follows: The Motion is granted in part and denied in part as follows:

The vehicle inspection shall occur no later than May 17, 2018 unless the parties stipulate to a different date. Plaintiff's counsel and Plaintiff's expert may attend the inspection to insure there will be no spoliation of evidence. Plaintiff shall only be entitled to the inspection data pursuant to the rules governing expert disclosure.

No sanctions.

Order signed. (See order filed this date for details.)

Judge Pro Tem: Thomas A. Cohen, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, David Disbrow, CSR #7768, Tel. 415.298.1538. (302/JPT/HEK)
